Collision hazard; crash costs life and 30 seconds

Sheer drop visible on mountain courses with cliff on roadside
danger_signals.meaning: Falling off results in immediate crash/life loss
Engine blow-up; results in life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Fuel depletion after passing gas station
danger_signals.meaning: Running out of gas before reaching next station results in life loss
Confined space increases collision risk with traffic

safety_signals.visual_form: Gas station at end of each course level
safety_signals.meaning: Mandatory stop point to refuel; safe destination

safety_signals.visual_form: Standard equipment on all selectable vehicles
safety_signals.meaning: Advance warning of police presence; allows speed adjustment to avoid pursuit
Staying under speed limit prevents police pursuit initiation

safety_signals.visual_form: Extra life granted for clean level completion
safety_signals.meaning: Successfully avoiding crashes and fuel depletion provides safety buffer

safety_signals.visual_form: Desert and grassy areas with clear sightlines
safety_signals.meaning: Lower collision density; safer driving terrain

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Female police officer character (arrests vs. smiles based on evasion success)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Police cars stationed along course routes; appear when speed limit exceeded

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Selectable exotic car (Porsche 959, Ferrari F40, Lamborghini Diablo in console)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player character shown in end sequence (smiling if evaded police, arrested if caught)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player vehicle starting position on roadway; racing against clock or AI opponent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Police car begins pursuit; radar detector signals alert
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Police AI pathfinds toward player vehicle; pursues if player maintains high speed

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player maintains legal speed near police
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Police car remains stationary or passes without engagement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Police ignore player; no pursuit initiated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Police car pulls ahead of player on roadway
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to pull over; receives ticket; time penalty applied

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Impact animation between player and police vehicle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game instantly ends; all lives forfeited regardless of remaining count

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Course completion without law enforcement contact
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Female police officer and player character on screen together, both smiling
Female police officer placing player character under arrest
Cacti along roadside; sandy environment; clear visibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Easy difficulty; lower traffic density; straightforward navigation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sheer rock wall on one side; cliff drop on other; tunnel passages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High collision risk; reduced driving options; tunnel sections increase danger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trees scattered along roadway; flat landscape; multiple lane highway
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Medium difficulty; tree collision hazard; off-road driving damage accumulation

environmental_storytelling.example: Mount Rushmore visible in 'City Bound' course establishes Midwest location; Seattle skyline and Space Needle in 'West Coast' course confirms West Coast setting
Desert has cacti; mountains have cliff edges and rock walls; grassy areas have trees — each creates location-specific danger context
Courses named 'Desert Blast (Easy)' through 'West Coast (Hardest)' with increasing length and complexity; establishes geographic progression across continental USA
Different courses feature varying amounts of oncoming traffic; creates environmental storytelling of road congestion in different regions
Police stationed throughout each course; reinforces theme of highway authority and legal risk in public road racing scenario
Presenting Porsche 959, Ferrari F40, and Lamborghini Diablo with technical specs (top speed, torque, acceleration) establishes high-performance racing context and player capability choice

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationed on roadway; no movement until player speed violation detected
Initiates pursuit; follows player vehicle; attempts to overtake
Overtakes player; forces pull-over; triggers ticket issuance and time penalty
Smiles at player character; indicates successful outcome
Places player character under arrest; indicates game loss
Move predictably on multi-lane highways; present static collision hazards
Consumes fuel rapidly; triggers police pursuit if exceeding limits; risks redline engine damage on manual transmission
Takes damage; crash adds 30-second penalty; sustains damage over time on flatland; life lost on contact with cliffs or obstacles

notes: Test Drive II: The Duel establishes a highway law enforcement dynamic as its primary world-state manifestation. The game's visual communication centers on the binary relationship between racer and police authority. Danger signals are primarily environmental (obstacles, terrain) and procedural (police presence). The female police officer serves as the key narrative entity whose behavior changes based on player evasion success — this creates a dramatic tension between achievement (speed racing) and consequence (legal authority). Console versions add geographic location storytelling through landmark visibility. The game's world-state is fundamentally driven by speed regulation mechanics: exceeding speed limits is mechanically required to win (courses are 'virtually impossible' to complete without police confrontation) yet visually dangerous, creating thematic irony between the racing objective and law enforcement consequence.
